[A21] FOODSTUFFS -- A21 Foodstuffs covers inventions relating to edible products and their preparation, including compositions, processing methods, preservation, and packaging of food items such as meat, fish, dairy, fruits, vegetables, and bakery goods, as well as additives, flavorings, and nutritional supplements used in food.
[A21B] BAKERS' OVENS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Industrial baker's ovens for baking food products;  Parts or accessories for such ovens, e.g. baker's peels and baking forms, tins, sheets or boards used in such ovens;  Baking apparatuses for specials goods, e.g. waffles, cups, cones, pancakes, doughnuts;  Baking plants and baking apparatuses in combination with mixing or kneading devices.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Domestic baking equipment -> A47J37/00  **Glossary:** - Baking: cooking food by dry heat acting by conduction note: normally in an oven, bus also in hot ashes, or on hot stones; baked food products are dough-based products like pastries, cakes or bread, or baked potatoes, baked apples, baked beans, etc.
[A21B 1/00] Bakers' ovens -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The bakers' ovens are classified according to the following aspects:
[A21B 1/02] characterised by the heating arrangements -- A21B 1/02 covers baker's ovens that are defined by their heating arrangements, meaning the specific placement, type or control of heat sources within the oven. This subgroup focuses on designs where the heating configuration is a distinguishing feature for achieving particular baking results.
[A21B 1/04] Ovens heated by fire before baking only -- A21B 1/04 relates to baker's ovens that are heated by fire only before the baking operation, without any additional heating during baking. This subclass includes traditional wood- or coal-fired ovens where the heat is applied in advance and then retained for the baking cycle.
[A21B 1/06] Ovens heated by radiators -- A21B 1/06 relates to bakers' ovens in which the heat is generated by radiators rather than direct flame or hot-air circulation. The classification covers designs that use a radiator system to distribute heat evenly for baking purposes.
[A21B 1/08] by steam-heated radiators -- A21B 1/08 refers to bakers' ovens that are heated by steam-heated radiators. This subclass covers oven designs in which steam-producing units provide the radiant heat for baking, distinguishing them from other heating methods such as direct fire or electric elements.
[A21B 1/10] by radiators heated by fluids other than steam -- A21B 1/10 covers bakers' ovens whose heating system uses radiators warmed by a fluid other than steam, such as hot water, oil or other heat-transfer liquids, to provide the baking temperature. This subclass distinguishes fluid-heated ovens from those powered by steam or direct combustion.
[A21B 1/14] Arrangement of radiators -- A21B 1/14 covers the specific layout and positioning of heating radiators within bakers' ovens, detailing how radiators are arranged to achieve uniform temperature distribution for baking processes. The classification includes designs that integrate radiators with oven chambers, mounts and airflow channels to optimize heat transfer and product quality.
[A21B 1/145] Radiators consisting of tubes -- A21B 1/145 covers bakers' ovens that use tubular radiators as the heating element, where heat is emitted from a series of metal tubes to bake or roast products. This sub-class is applied to ovens whose temperature control relies on the radiation from these tube-based units.
[A21B 1/22] by electric radiators -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Baking apparatus employing high-frequency or infrared heating -> A21B2/00
[A21B 1/24] Ovens heated by media flowing therethrough -- A21B 1/24 covers bakers' ovens in which the heating is provided by a fluid such as steam, hot water, oil or other media that is pumped or otherwise caused to flow through the oven cavity, supplying heat directly to the baked product. This type of oven is used for uniform, controllable baking in commercial bakeries.
[A21B 1/245] with a plurality of air nozzles to obtain an impingement effect on the food -- A21B 1/245 covers bakers' ovens that use several air nozzles to direct a jet of air onto the food, creating an impingement effect that improves heat transfer and cooking uniformity. This subclass falls under Human Necessities → Foodstuffs → Bakers' Ovens in the CPC system.
[A21B 1/26] by hot air -- A21B 1/26 classifies bakers' ovens that bake by circulating hot air, i.e., convection or hot-air ovens used in food preparation. It covers devices where a fan or similar mechanism distributes heated air throughout the baking chamber to achieve uniform cooking. This subclass falls under Human Necessities → Foodstuffs → Bakers' Ovens in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[A21B 1/28] by gaseous combustion products -- A21B 1/28 covers bakers' ovens that are heated by gaseous combustion products such as natural gas, propane or butane. The subclass includes designs where the flame or hot gases are directed onto the baking chamber to provide the required temperature for bread, pastries or other bakery products.
[A21B 1/33] Ovens heated directly by combustion products -- This subclass of the CPC covers baker's ovens that obtain heat directly from the flame or hot gases produced by combustion of a fuel, without intermediary heat exchangers. It includes designs where the combustion products flow through the baking chamber or are otherwise applied directly to the baked goods.
[A21B 1/36] Ovens heated directly by hot fluid -- A21B 1/36 refers to baker's ovens that are heated directly by a hot fluid, such as steam, hot water, oil or another heated liquid, which transfers heat to the oven cavity without an intermediate combustion chamber. This subclass includes designs where the fluid circulates through or contacts the baking space to provide the required temperature for baking.
[A21B 1/40] characterised by the means for regulating the temperature -- A21B 1/40 covers bakers' ovens that include means for regulating temperature, such as thermostats, thermostatic controls or other temperature-control devices. This subclass falls under the broader categories of human necessities, foodstuffs and bakers' ovens within the CPC system.
[A21B 1/42] characterised by the baking surfaces moving during the baking -- A21B 1/42 covers bakers' ovens in which the baking surfaces themselves move while the product is being baked, such as conveyors, rotating shelves or shaking trays, to promote even heat distribution and consistent browning of the baked goods.
[A21B 1/44] with surfaces rotating in a horizontal plane -- A21B 1/44 covers bakers' ovens that have cooking surfaces rotating in a horizontal plane, such as racks or trays turning around a vertical axis. The rotating surfaces move the dough while it bakes, providing more even heat exposure and higher throughput.
[A21B 1/46] with surfaces suspended from an endless conveyor or a revolving wheel -- This subgroup of bakers' ovens covers ovens in which the cooking surfaces are carried on a continuously moving belt or a rotating wheel, allowing food to be processed while the surfaces travel through the heating zone.
[A21B 1/48] with surfaces in the form of an endless band -- A21B 1/48 covers bakers' ovens whose interior cooking surfaces are formed as an endless band, typically a continuous belt or drum that moves the product through the heating zone for uniform baking. This design enables continuous processing of bread, pastries, or similar goods, improving throughput and consistent product quality. It falls under the Human Necessities → Foodstuffs → Bakers' Ovens hierarchy in the CPC system.
[A21B 1/50] characterised by having removable baking surfaces -- Bakers' ovens characterised by having removable baking surfaces are classified under A21B 1/50 in the CPC system, covering ovens where the baking pans or trays can be detached for cleaning or replacement, facilitating flexibility in baking operations.
[A21B 1/52] Portable ovens -- A21B 1/52 covers portable ovens designed for baking applications, encompassing movable or hand-carried devices that can be used on-site for bread, pastry or other foodstuff preparation, and that retain the functional characteristics of fixed bakers' ovens while offering transportability.
[A21B 2/00] Baking apparatus employing high-frequency or infrared heating -- A21B 2/00 covers baking equipment that uses electromagnetic energy, such as high-frequency microwaves or infrared radiation, as the main heat source. It includes ovens and similar apparatus where the heating is provided by these radiative methods rather than conventional hot-air convection.
[A21B 3/00] Parts or accessories of ovens -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[A21B 3/003] Baker's peels -- A21B 3/003 covers baker's peels, the flat, often wooden or metal tools used to lift, place, and retrieve loaves and trays in baker's ovens. They are classified as parts or accessories of ovens within the foodstuffs category of human necessities.
[A21B 3/006] Means for cleaning the baking cavity -- A21B 3/006 includes devices integrated into or attached to bakers' ovens that remove residue, ash or char from the baking cavity, such as scrapers, brushes or self-cleaning mechanisms. These means facilitate maintenance and help keep baking performance consistent.
[A21B 3/02] Doors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details of doors and flap gates that provide access to the baking cavity of industrial baking ovens. A flap gate is an access opening comprising a flap which is pivotable on its upper or lower edge. Such access means are generally used in stacked baking ovens. Furthermore, this group covers details of doors of bakers' ovens, e.g. ovens wherein a movable trolley loaded with food products is rolled into the baking cavity or ovens wherein the baking surfaces move during the baking.
[A21B 3/04] Air-treatment devices for ovens, e.g. regulating humidity -- A21B 3/04 covers devices that treat the air inside bakers' ovens, such as systems that add, remove or control moisture to maintain optimal humidity levels during baking. These air-treatment solutions improve product quality and consistency by regulating the oven's internal atmosphere.
[A21B 3/07] Charging or discharging ovens -- A21B 3/07 covers devices and methods for loading or unloading bakers' ovens, including mechanisms that introduce or remove product, heat, or steam during operation. It pertains to accessories that facilitate charging or discharging the oven chamber.
[A21B 3/10] Means for illuminating ovens -- A21B 3/10 refers to devices that provide light inside bakers' ovens, such as built-in lamps, light fixtures or transparent windows used to illuminate the cooking chamber for monitoring the baking process. The classification includes any illumination system integrated into or attached to the oven structure.
[A21B 3/13] Baking-tins -- A21B 3/13 covers baking tins, the metal pans used in ovens for shaping and cooking baked goods. These are components or accessories for bakers' ovens, designed to hold dough or batter during baking.
[A21B 3/131] removable, foldable or disposable -- A21B 3/131 pertains to removable, foldable or disposable parts or accessories for bakers' ovens, such as trays, racks, pans, doors or shelves that can be taken out, collapsed for storage or used only once. These components are designed to facilitate cleaning, transport or single-use applications in commercial baking equipment.
[A21B 3/132] Assemblies of several baking-tins or forms  A21B3/134  takes precedence -- It covers devices that join two or more baking tins or forms into a single unit, such as stacked, interlocking or otherwise connected tin assemblies used in baker's ovens. The classification is for combined tray or mold assemblies rather than individual tins and falls under parts or accessories of ovens.
[A21B 3/133] for making bread -- A21B 3/133 pertains to parts or accessories specifically designed for ovens used to make bread, such as dough mixers, baking trays, steam injectors, temperature controls and other devices that facilitate the baking process within bakers' ovens.
[A21B 3/134] Multiple bread pans -- A21B 3/134 covers parts or accessories for bakers' ovens that consist of multiple bread pans used together, such as linked, stacked, or otherwise arranged pans for batch baking. It includes designs that hold several pans simultaneously to increase throughput in commercial baking.
[A21B 3/135] Accessories, e.g. covers, handles -- A21B 3/135 pertains to accessories for bakers' ovens, including items such as protective covers, handles, and other detachable components that aid in operation, cleaning, or safety. These parts are intended to be fitted to or removed from the oven as needed.
[A21B 3/136] with reinforcements -- A21B 3/136 covers parts or accessories for bakers' ovens that incorporate reinforcements, such as strengthened shelves, trays, or walls designed to withstand high temperatures and heavy loads during baking operations.
[A21B 3/137] with detachable side and bottom parts, e.g. springform -- A21B 3/137 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to bakers' oven components that feature detachable side and bottom parts, such as those found in a springform pan, designed for easy removal of baked goods.
[A21B 3/138] flexible forms, e.g. made from silicone -- This subclass covers flexible molds or forms, typically silicone, designed as removable accessories for bakers' ovens. They enable shaping, releasing or supporting baked products such as breads, pastries or confectionery within the oven cavity.
[A21B 3/139] with apertures to vent steam -- A21B 3/139 refers to parts or accessories of bakers' ovens that include apertures designed to vent steam, helping control moisture during baking.
[A21B 3/15] Baking sheets -- A21B 3/15 relates to baking sheets, the flat metal trays placed in bakers' ovens for cooking breads, pastries and other foodstuffs. These sheets are classified as parts or accessories of ovens within the human necessities-foodstuffs hierarchy.
[A21B 3/155] of wire or mesh wire material  A21B3/131  takes precedence -- A21B 3/155 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to parts or accessories of bakers' ovens made of wire or mesh wire material, with A21B 3/131 taking precedence if applicable.
[A21B 3/16] Machines for cleaning or greasing baking surfaces -- A21B 3/16 relates to equipment used to clean or grease the interior surfaces of bakers' ovens, including spray devices, rollers, brushes or similar machines that maintain a non-stick baking environment.
[A21B 3/18] Discharging baked goods from tins -- A21B 3/18 covers mechanisms that discharge baked goods from their tins, including ejector rods, push plates, pneumatic or hydraulic devices and other accessories that automate the removal of loaves, pastries or cakes from a baker's oven without manual handling.
[A21B 5/00] Baking apparatus for special goods -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Baking apparatuses for special goods, e.g. hollow articles, waffles, pastry, biscuits, pancakes, or tortillas. These machines are used to produce a high quantity of baked goods. Domestic kitchen equipment, e.g. waffle-irons comprising hinged moulds are classified in A47J37/0611 .
[A21B 5/02] Apparatus for baking hollow articles, waffles, pastry, biscuits, or the like -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Domestic waffle-irons -> A47J37/0611
[A21B 5/023] Hinged moulds for baking waffles -- A21B 5/023 covers hinged molds designed for baking waffles, a specialized accessory used in bakers' ovens to shape and release the waffle batter. The classification pertains to equipment that folds or opens to facilitate removal of the finished waffle while preserving its form.
[A21B 5/026] for baking waffle cups or cones -- A21B 5/026 covers devices designed to bake waffle cups or cones, a subcategory of bakers' ovens within foodstuff apparatus. It includes specialized baking equipment that shapes batter into cup or cone forms, typically used for waffles, ice-cream cones or similar products.
[A21B 5/03] for baking pancakes -- A21B 5/03 pertains to equipment or methods specifically designed for baking pancakes, a thin, flat batter product. It classifies appliances or processes that prepare this particular type of quick-cooked bakery good within the bakers' ovens subgroup of the foodstuff hierarchy.
[A21B 5/04] Apparatus for baking cylindrical cakes on spits -- A21B 5/04 covers devices for baking cylindrical cakes on rotating spits inside bakers' ovens, including mechanisms that hold, turn and support the cakes as they bake, ensuring even heat exposure. This sub-category falls under the broader groups for bakers' ovens and special-goods baking apparatus.
[A21B 5/06] Apparatus for baking in salt solution, e.g. for making pretzels -- A21B 5/06 refers to baking equipment that uses a salt solution during the cooking process, a method typically employed to produce pretzels. The apparatus integrates a baking chamber with a saline bath to create the distinctive crust and flavor associated with such baked goods.
[A21B 5/08] Apparatus for baking in baking fat or oil, e.g. for making doughnuts -- A21B 5/08 is a subclass of the CPC group for bakers' ovens, covering devices that bake products directly in fat or oil rather than in dry heat. It includes machines such as doughnut-making apparatus that submerge the dough in heated oil to produce fried bakery items.
[A21B 5/085] for applying oil to bakery products -- A21B 5/085 covers apparatuses that apply oil to bakery products, such as sprayers, brushes or oil-distribution units used during or after baking to improve crust colour, flavour or texture. These devices are classified under bakers' ovens within the broader foodstuffs group.
[A21B 7/00] Baking plants -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Systems comprising a dough rising chamber with handling means to transport dough products into or through a baking cavity;  Systems comprising a (plurality of) baking chamber(s) connected to a central control and monitoring device;  Baking apparatus, e.g. for bread, in combination with mixing or kneading devices
[A21B 7/005] in combination with mixing or kneading devices -- A21B 7/005 relates to baker's ovens that are integrated with mixing or kneading equipment, allowing dough preparation and baking to occur in a single, combined unit within a baking plant. The subclass covers arrangements where mixers or kneaders are directly linked to the oven to streamline production.
[A21C] MACHINES OR EQUIPMENT FOR MAKING OR PROCESSING DOUGHS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines or Equipment, including e.g. hand tools or trays, for preparing, shaping and processing edible dough, namely for mixing, kneading, dividing, working, forming, shaping, cutting, proving, handling the dough before the baking, cooking or prebaking process with optional subsequent freezing.  Machines or equipment, including hand tools, for handling baked or prebaked articles made from edible dough.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Dough and dough or bakery products as such, e.g. chemical treatment or recipes -> A21D - Pasta and noodles as such -> A23L7/109  **Glossary:** - Dough: is understood to be an edible substance being baked before consumption. The dough can be in a semi-liquid state, e.g. batter, or the dough can be a pasty.
[A21C 1/00] Mixing or kneading machines for the preparation of dough -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for the mixing and/or kneading of dough, this preparation only concerning the mechanical aspects of it, i.e. the mixing of the individual components to obtain a homogeneous dough and/or its subsequent mechanical kneading to achieve the desired consistency.
[A21C 1/003] Plant; Systems, i.e. flow charts or diagrams -- A21C 1/003 covers the documentation of the plant and control systems for dough-mixing or kneading equipment, including flow charts, diagrams and schematics that show material and energy pathways, control loops and overall process layout for these machines.
[A21C 1/006] Methods -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purely mechanical methods for operating a dough mixing or kneading machine.  Example:
[A21C 1/02] with vertically-mounted tools -- A21C 1/02 covers mixing or kneading machines for dough that have tools mounted vertically, such as vertically oriented shafts, paddles or hooks, used to lift and fold the dough during processing. The classification belongs to the CPC group for equipment used in foodstuff preparation, specifically for dough handling.
[A21C 1/04] with inclined rotating mixing arms or levers -- This subgroup covers dough mixers that use inclined rotating arms or levers to fold and develop the dough. It is a subcategory of machines for making or processing dough under the foodstuff hierarchy.
[A21C 1/06] with horizontally-mounted mixing or kneading tools -- A21C 1/06 covers dough-mixing or kneading machines in which the mixing or kneading tools are mounted horizontally, such as rollers or paddles that rotate or oscillate on a horizontal axis to combine ingredients and develop dough. This subclass falls under machines for making or processing dough within the broader foodstuffs category.
[A21C 1/065] Worm or screw mixers, e.g. with consecutive mixing receptacles -- Worm or screw mixers are dough-mixing machines that use a rotating helical screw to convey and knead the dough while it passes through one or more successive mixing chambers. The design provides continuous mixing and gradual development of dough structure, making it suitable for industrial bakeries.
[A21C 1/08] with rollers -- A21C 1/08 covers mixing or kneading machines for dough that use rollers as the primary working element. The class includes roller mixers, roller kneaders and similar equipment where the dough is flattened, stretched or combined by rotating or reciprocating rollers.
[A21C 1/083] by calendering, i.e. working between rollers  using rollers for shaping dough  A21C3/02 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  For example:
[A21C 1/086] having a receptacle rotating about a vertical axis cooperating with rotary rollers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  For example:
[A21C 1/10] with additional aerating apparatus for the manufacture of aerated doughs -- A21C 1/10 covers mixing or kneading machines that incorporate an extra aerating device to produce aerated doughs, such as those used for light breads or pastries. The classification addresses equipment designed to introduce air while the dough is being mixed or kneaded.
[A21C 11/00] Other machines for forming the dough into its final shape before cooking or baking -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines not covered in A21C3/00 - A21C9/00 performing the final shaping or slitting of the dough prior to baking or cooking, of e.g. pasta.
[A21C 11/002] the shape being toroid-like, e.g. bagels, doughnuts  extruding machines for toroid shaped products  A21C11/166 -- A21C 11/002 covers machines that form dough into toroid-like shapes such as bagels or doughnuts, using extrusion or other processes to produce the final product before baking. It falls under the broader category of equipment for shaping dough into its final form.
[A21C 11/004] forming the dough into a substantially disc-like shape with or without an outer rim, e.g. for making pie crusts, cake shells or pizza bases -- The entry covers machines that shape dough into a substantially disc-like form, with or without an outer rim, for products such as pie crusts, cake shells or pizza bases. It falls under equipment for making or processing dough before cooking or baking.
[A21C 11/006] by pressing or press-moulding  A21C11/008  takes precedence -- A21C 11/006 refers to machines that form dough into its final shape by
[A21C 11/008] by press-rolling -- A21C 11/008 covers machines that form dough into its final shape by press-rolling, where the dough is fed through rollers that flatten or shape it into sheets or specific profiles before cooking or baking. These devices belong to the CPC group for equipment that shapes dough after mixing.
[A21C 11/02] Embossing machines -- Embossing machines are specialized equipment for shaping dough by impressing patterns or designs onto it before cooking or baking, enhancing texture, appearance and product variety in the bakery industry.
[A21C 11/04] with cutting and embossing rollers or drums -- This subclass covers machines that shape dough into its final form before baking, using cutting and embossing rollers or drums to divide, decorate, or imprint the product. The equipment is intended for continuous or batch processing of dough pieces, producing items such as biscuits, crackers, or patterned bakery goods.
[A21C 1/105] the gas being introduced through the shaft of a rotary tool -- A21C 1/105 covers dough-mixing or-kneading equipment in which a gas-such as air, steam or nitrogen-is fed through the rotating shaft of the tool to assist the preparation process, typically to aerate, cool or control temperature of the dough during mixing.
[A21C 11/06] handling regularly pre-shaped sheets of dough -- Machines or equipment for handling dough sheets that are already formed into regular shapes, such as cutting, transferring, stacking or aligning these sheets before they are cooked or baked, are classified under A21C 11/06. This subcategory falls within the broader group for machines that shape dough into its final form. It pertains specifically to the manipulation of pre-shaped dough sheets rather than the initial forming process.
[A21C 11/08] with engraved moulds, e.g. rotary machines with die rolls -- A21C 11/08 covers machines that form dough into its final shape by means of engraved moulds, such as rotary equipment equipped with die rolls. These devices shape dough before cooking or baking, falling under the broader category of equipment for making or processing dough.
[A21C 11/10] combined with cutting apparatus -- A21C 11/10 covers machines that shape dough into its final form and integrate a cutting mechanism to portion the product before cooking or baking. It applies to equipment where forming and slicing functions are combined in a single unit.
[A21C 11/103] having multiple cutting elements slidably or rotably mounted in a diaphragm-like arrangement -- This CPC subclass A21C 11/103 covers machines that form dough into its final shape and feature multiple cutting elements mounted in a diaphragm-like arrangement that can slide or rotate. It resides under Human Necessities, Foodstuffs, and Machines or equipment for making or processing dough.
[A21C 11/106] using hand-operated cutting tools  A21C9/068  takes precedence -- A21C 11/106 covers machines that shape dough into its final form before cooking or baking when the shaping is done using hand-operated cutting tools, and it is subordinated to the higher-priority subclass A21C9/068.
[A21C 11/12] Apparatus for slotting, slitting or perforating the surface of pieces of dough -- This subclass covers machines that create slots, slits or perforations in dough pieces as a final shaping step before cooking or baking, such as rollers, knives or punches designed to slot, slit or perforate the surface of the dough. It falls under equipment for making or processing dough.
[A21C 11/14] for star-like stamping -- A21C 11/14 relates to machines that shape dough by applying a star-like stamping pattern, forming the final shape of the product before it is cooked or baked.
[A21C 11/16] Extruding machines -- A21C 11/16 covers extruding machines that shape dough into its final form before cooking or baking, including equipment that forces dough through dies to produce continuous strands, sheets or specific profiles.
[A21C 11/163] Applying co-extrusion, i.e. extruding two or more plastic substances simultaneously, e.g. for making filled dough products; Making products from two or more different substances supplied to the extruder -- This CPC subclass covers machines that apply co-extrusion, extruding two or more plastic substances simultaneously to produce filled dough products, where different substances are supplied separately to the extruder. It falls under equipment for shaping dough before cooking or baking.
[A21C 11/166] for making toroid-shaped products, e.g. doughnuts  A21C11/163  takes precedence -- A21C 11/166 covers machines that shape dough into toroid-shaped products such as doughnuts, forming the ring before cooking or baking. This subclass refines A21C 11/163, which addresses general dough-forming equipment.
[A21C 11/18] with pistons -- A21C 11/18 covers dough-forming machines that use pistons to shape the product, such as equipment that pushes or extrudes dough into its final form before it is cooked or baked. It falls under the hierarchy of human necessities → foodstuffs → machines or equipment for making or processing dough → other machines for forming the dough into its final shape.
[A21C 1/12] for the preparation of dough directly from grain -- Mixing or kneading machines that form dough directly from grain combine grain processing, such as milling, with dough preparation in a single apparatus, allowing dough to be produced without intermediate flour handling. This equipment is used in bakeries and food-processing plants to streamline the production of bread, pastries and similar products.
[A21C 11/20] with worms -- A21C 11/20 covers dough-shaping machines that use a worm or worm-gear mechanism to feed and form the product into its final shape before baking. The classification applies to equipment where a helical screw or worm drives the dough through shaping dies or rollers. It is a subcategory of machines for processing dough within the broader foodstuffs group.
[A21C 11/22] Apparatus with rollers and cutting discs or blades for shaping noodles -- This subclass covers machines that shape dough into noodle forms using a combination of rollers and cutting discs or blades, forming the final noodle shape before cooking or baking. It includes equipment that continuously extrudes, cuts and shapes the dough into strands or other noodle profiles.
[A21C 11/24] Apparatus for cutting out noodles from a sheet or ribbon of dough by an engaging pair of grooved rollers -- A machine that forms noodle strands by feeding a sheet or ribbon of dough through a pair of engaged grooved rollers, the rollers' complementary cuts separating the dough into the desired shape before cooking or baking.
[A21C 1/14] Structural elements of mixing or kneading machines; -- A21C 1/14 covers the structural components of dough-mixing or kneading machines, such as frames, shafts, bearings, gears and other parts that support and transmit the mixing action. It defines the mechanical architecture of equipment used in the preparation of dough within the broader category of foodstuff processing machinery.
[A21C 1/1405] Tools -- A21C 1/1405 covers the various tools employed in mixing or kneading machines used to prepare dough, such as blades, paddles, cutters, or other implements that shape, divide or handle the dough during processing. These components are integral to equipment that mixes or kneads foodstuffs in the dough-making stage of food production.
[A21C 1/141] having mixing or cutting elements essentially perpendicular to their axes -- A21C 1/141 designates mixing or cutting elements that are oriented essentially perpendicular to their rotation axes in dough-mixing or kneading machines. The classification covers machines where blades or hooks intersect the dough at right angles to the shaft,
[A21C 1/1415] Shafts; Heads therefor -- The entry A21C 1/1415 covers shafts and their heads used in mixing or kneading machines for dough. It relates to the components that transmit drive power to the mixing elements and provide support or coupling within the equipment. This classification groups any specific shaft designs, connections, or head features employed in dough-processing machinery.
[A21C 1/142] Feeding mechanisms, e.g. skip lifting mechanisms -- A21C 1/142 relates to feeding mechanisms for dough-mixing or kneading machines, such as skip-lifting devices that transfer dough from a storage bin to the processing area. These mechanisms are part of equipment used in preparing foodstuff dough.
[A21C 1/1425] for feeding in measured doses -- A21C 1/1425 pertains to mixing or kneading machines for dough preparation that include a system for feeding ingredients in measured doses, allowing precise addition of components during the mixing process.
[A21C 1/143] using pneumatic means -- A21C 1/143 covers dough-mixing or kneading apparatus that employ pneumatic means, such as air pressure or vacuum, to manipulate the dough during processing. The classification applies to machines designed for preparing food where air-driven mechanisms are used to achieve mixing, kneading or shaping of dough.
[A21C 1/1435] for liquids  A21C1/1425  takes precedence -- A21C 1/1435 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to mixing or kneading machines specifically designed for handling liquids, with a note that A21C1/1425 takes precedence, indicating a more specific classification for certain liquid processing methods.
[A21C 1/144] Discharge mechanisms -- A21C 1/144 covers the discharge mechanisms used in mixing or kneading machines for preparing dough, such as augers, blades, flaps or other devices that remove the dough from the machine after processing. This subclass falls within the CPC hierarchy for human necessities, foodstuffs, and equipment for making or processing dough.
[A21C 1/1445] using tiltable receptacles; Tilting mechanisms therefor -- The entry covers dough-mixing or kneading machines that employ receptacles which can be inclined, and the mechanisms that tilt those receptacles to improve ingredient incorporation or discharge. It is classified under machines for making or processing dough, specifically the subcategory dealing with tiltable bowls and their tilting devices.
[A21C 1/145] Controlling; Testing; Measuring  A21C1/1425  takes precedence -- A21C 1/145 covers the control, testing and measurement functions of dough mixing or kneading equipment, such as sensors, feedback systems and performance monitoring devices integrated into or applied to the machines. It falls under the broader class A21C for dough-processing machines
[A21C 1/1455] Measuring data of the driving system, e.g. torque, speed, power -- This CPC subclass covers devices that monitor and record operating parameters of dough-mixing or kneading machines, such as torque, speed and power of the driving system. It includes measurement and control systems integrated into the equipment to optimise performance.
[A21C 1/146] Measuring properties of the dough, e.g. moisture, electrical conductivity, temperature  A21C1/1455  takes precedence -- A21C 1/146 relates to the measurement of dough characteristics such as moisture content, electrical conductivity and temperature while the dough is being mixed or kneaded. It falls under machines or equipment for making or processing dough and is subordinate to A21C 1/1455.
[A21C 1/1465] Drives -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Wind motors -> F03D - Hydraulic motors -> F15B - Brakes -> F16D - Couplings -> F16D - Transmissions -> F16G , F16H - Electric motors -> H02K
[A21C 1/147] for planetary motion -- This subclass covers dough-mixing or kneading machines that employ planetary motion, where the working element rotates on its own axis while simultaneously revolving around a central axis to achieve uniform processing. It is placed under machines for making or processing dough within the foodstuffs category.
[A21C 1/1475] Brakes therefor -- A21C 1/1475 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to brakes used in mixing or kneading machines designed for preparing dough, ensuring precise control and safety during operation.
[A21C 1/148] Safety arrangements  A21C1/1485  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Doors; Closures; Operating, e.g. safety, mechanisms of mixing or kneading machines -> A21C1/1485 - Safety devices in general -> F16P
[A21C 1/1485] Doors; Closures; Operating, e.g. safety, mechanisms therefor -- The CPC entry A21C 1/1485 covers doors, closures and their operating mechanisms-including safety devices-for machines that mix or knead dough, relating to the way such equipment is accessed, sealed or controlled during food-stuff processing.
[A21C 1/149] Receptacles, e.g. provided with means for carrying or guiding fluids, e.g. coolants -- The subclass A21C 1/149 covers receptacles incorporated in dough-mixing or kneading machines that include features for carrying or guiding fluids, such as coolants, through the equipment. It pertains to containers or passages designed to deliver or circulate cooling or lubricating liquids within the machine's processing area.
[A21C 1/1495] Arrangements for cooling or heating  receptacles with means for guiding fluids  per se   A21C1/149 ; Arrangements for applying super- or sub-atmospheric pressure -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Heating or cooling elements in combination with the mixing device, the specific details of the heat-exchange means not being of relevance.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Receptacles with means for guiding fluids per se -> A21C1/149 - Heat exchangers in general -> F28
[A21C 13/00] Provers, i.e. apparatus permitting dough to rise -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Climate controlled spaces or chambers for dough.
[A21C 13/02] with endless conveyors -- A21C 13/02 refers to dough provers that incorporate endless-conveyor systems, allowing dough to move continuously through a controlled proofing environment. These machines belong to the category of equipment for making or processing dough, specifically designed to facilitate uniform rising by transporting the product on a looped belt or chain.
[A21C 14/00] Machines or equipment for making or processing dough, not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines or equipment for making or processing dough, not provided for in other groups of this subclass, e.g. apparatus, devices and hand tools used for marking dough.
[A21C 15/00] Apparatus for handling baked articles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus for handling and refining baked articles.  Apparatus for specifically cutting baked articles other than bread.
[A21C 15/002] Apparatus for spreading granular material on, or sweeping or coating the surface of baked articles  of pieces of dough  A21C9/04 ; for confectionary  A23G3/28 -- A21C 15/002 covers equipment used to spread, sweep or coat granular substances onto the surfaces of baked dough pieces, such as breads, rolls or pastries, and includes similar devices for applying toppings to confectionery items. The apparatus typically handles powders, sugars, seeds or other fine particles during production.
[A21C 15/005] of which at least the dispensing part is hand-held, e.g. comprising a flexible container, pouch or gun-like applicator -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus for coating the surface of baked articles comprising a stationary part, e.g. a tank and a hand-held dispensing part, e.g. a gun-like applicator.  Apparatus for coating the surface of baked articles being entirely hand-held.
[A21C 15/007] Apparatus for filling baked articles  filling pieces of dough  A21C9/06 ; filled bakery products  A21D13/30 ,  A21D13/80 -- A21C 15/007 covers equipment used to handle baked goods for the purpose of filling them, such as machines that insert dough pieces or other fillings into breads, pastries or similar products, and is linked to related sub-entries for filled bakery items.
[A21C 15/02] Apparatus for shaping or moulding baked wafers -- **Glossary:** - Wafer: any sheet like baked product being, at ambient temperature, either rigid or soft
[A21C 15/025] Apparatus for shaping or moulding baked wafers, e.g. to obtain cones for ice cream -- A21C 15/025 concerns apparatus for shaping or moulding baked wafers, such as devices that form wafer cones for ice-cream. The equipment handles baked articles and forms them into specific shapes for confectionery use. It falls within the subclass of machines for making or processing dough under the human necessities category.
[A21C 15/04] Cutting or slicing machines or devices specially adapted for baked articles other than bread -- The A21C 15/04 subclass covers cutting or slicing machines and devices specially adapted for baked articles other than bread, such as pastries, cakes, biscuits and similar products. These apparatuses are designed to handle, portion, or slice baked goods efficiently in commercial or industrial settings.
[A21C 3/00] Machines or apparatus for shaping batches of dough before subdivision -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines for shaping dough, supplied in batches or continuously, to a semi-finished product, followed by subdividing and/or another shaping step.  Machines for twisting discrete strips of dough to obtain the final shape, e.g. for making a pretzel.  Machines for twisting strips of dough followed by subdividing to obtain the final shape.  Machines for coiling sheets of dough to obtain the final shape or followed by subdividing.
[A21C 3/02] Dough-sheeters -- A dough-sheeter is a machine that flattens batches of dough into continuous sheets of uniform thickness, preparing the material for subsequent subdivision into portions such as pastry or bread. It belongs to the class of equipment for shaping dough before cutting.
[A21C 3/021] Rolling-pins; Hand-operated spreading or rolling devices  A21C11/004  takes precedence -- A21C 3/021 covers machines or apparatus that shape batches of dough prior to division, specifically hand-operated rolling-pins and similar spreading or rolling devices used to flatten and form dough before it is cut or portioned. This subclass falls under the broader category of equipment for processing dough within the foodstuff sector.
[A21C 3/022] Laminating or undulating a continuous dough sheet, e.g. by folding transversely or longitudinally onto a moving surface  combined with filling  A21C9/045 -- A21C 3/022 covers methods and apparatus for laminating or undulating a continuous dough sheet, such as transverse or longitudinal folding onto a moving surface, often combined with adding filling, in machines that shape dough batches before they are divided.
[A21C 3/024] using one or more rollers with an axis of rotation substantially parallel or oblique to the direction of dough transport  A21C3/065  takes precedence -- A21C 3/024 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers machines that shape batches of dough before subdivision, specifically those using one or more rollers with an axis of rotation parallel or oblique to the direction of dough transport. However, A21C 3/065 takes precedence when applicable.
[A21C 3/025] with one or more rollers moving perpendicularly to its rotation axis, e.g. reciprocally  A21C3/024  takes precedence; hand-operated rollers  A21C3/021 -- The A21C 3/025 subclass covers machines or apparatus that shape batches of dough using one or more rollers that move perpendicular to the rollers' rotation axis, such as reciprocating rollers. It is subordinated to A21C 3/024 and excludes hand-operated rollers defined in A21C 3/021.
[A21C 3/027] with multiple rollers moving in a closed loop, e.g. in an orbital path; Planetary roller systems -- A21C 3/027 covers machines that shape dough batches using several rollers that travel in a closed loop, such as planetary roller configurations where the rollers rotate around a common centre while turning the dough.
[A21C 3/028] using rollers having a shape other than straight round cylinders  A21C3/04  takes precedence -- A21C 3/028 covers machines or apparatus that shape batches of dough before it is divided, employing rollers whose cross-section differs from straight round cylinders. It falls under Human Necessities → Foodstuffs → Machines or equipment for making or processing doughs, with the broader A21C 3/04 class taking precedence.
[A21C 3/04] Dough-extruding machines -- A21C 3/04 pertains to dough-extruding machines that shape continuous batches of dough into a desired profile before the material is divided into portions. The devices typically use a screw or similar mechanism to force the dough through a die, producing uniform strands or shapes for further processing.
[A21C 3/06] Machines for coiling sheets of dough, e.g. for producing rolls -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This place also covers crescent-rolls.
[A21C 3/065] the coil axis of the produced rolls being in parallel or oblique position relative to the transport direction; with coiling elements, e.g. rollers, being placed in parallel or oblique position relative to the transport direction -- A21C 3/065 covers dough-shaping machines that coil the finished rolls with the roll axis aligned parallel to, or at an oblique angle from, the direction of product transport. The classification also applies when the coiling rollers themselves are positioned parallel to or at an oblique angle to the transport path.
[A21C 3/08] Machines for twisting strips of dough, e.g. for making pretzels -- A21C 3/08 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers machines specifically designed for twisting strips of dough, such as those used in making pretzels or similar twisted bread products. This classification is part of a broader category that includes various machines and equipment for processing dough before it is subdivided into individual pieces.
[A21C 3/10] combined with dough-dividing apparatus -- A21C 3/10 covers machines that shape a batch of dough and incorporate a dough-dividing unit, enabling continuous forming and portioning of dough in bakeries or food-processing plants. The apparatus typically combines rollers, molds or sheeters with cutters or slicers to produce uniformly sized pieces ready for baking.
[A21C 5/00] Dough-dividing machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines for dividing batches of dough or batter, i.e. semi-liquid dough, into equally sized portions.
[A21C 5/003] with revolving bodies, e.g. with bodies having spaces separated by radially oriented elements or walls -- A21C 5/003 covers dough-dividing machines that use rotating bodies whose compartments are separated by radially oriented elements or walls. The classification applies to equipment where the dough is split as the revolving sections create distinct spaces, facilitating consistent portioning during dough processing.
[A21C 5/006] for dispensing liquid or semi-liquid dough, e.g. batter -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Dough-dividing machines for dividing liquid or semi-liquid dough by intermittently dispensing.
[A21C 5/02] with division boxes and ejection plungers -- A21C 5/02 covers dough-dividing machines that incorporate division boxes and ejection plungers, used to separate dough into portions during processing. These devices belong to the foodstuff equipment group for making or processing doughs, a subcategory of machines for handling human necessities.
[A21C 5/04] with division boxes in a revolving body with radially-working pistons -- A21C 5/04 refers to dough-dividing machines that incorporate division boxes arranged in a revolving body and use pistons that operate radially to cut and portion the dough.
[A21C 5/06] with division boxes in a revolving body with axially-working pistons -- A dough-dividing machine that incorporates division boxes mounted in a rotating body where pistons act along the axis to separate the dough. The design enables continuous, uniform portioning of dough for bakery or food-processing operations, fitting within the CPC group for machines used to make or process doughs.
[A21C 5/08] with radial, i.e. star-like, cutter-blades slidable in the slots of, and relatively to, a pressing-plunger -- A21C 5/08 covers dough-dividing machines in which a pressing plunger carries radial, star-like cutter blades that slide within slots of the plunger, enabling the dough to be cut by the rotating blades as the plunger descends. This arrangement provides a compact, adjustable cut for bakery processing.
[A21C 7/00] Machines which homogenise the subdivided dough by working other than by kneading -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines for working or rounding discrete dough portions.
[A21C 7/005] the dough pieces being worked in radially disposed cavities in a rotating drum -- This subclass defines machines that homogenise subdivided dough by passing the pieces through radially arranged cavities in a rotating drum, operating by a method other than kneading. It applies to equipment used in dough processing within the broader foodstuff and human necessities categories.
[A21C 7/01] with endless bands -- A21C 7/01 covers dough-homogenising machines that use continuous, endless bands-such as belts or conveyors-to blend subdivided dough rather than employing kneading actions. These devices fall under the broader category of equipment for making or processing doughs within the foodstuffs group.
[A21C 7/02] with moulding channels -- A21C 7/02 refers to machines that shape dough by passing it through moulding channels, homogenising the subdivided dough through actions other than kneading.
[A21C 7/04] with moulding cups -- A21C 7/04 refers to machines that homogenise subdivided dough by means other than kneading, using moulding cups to shape and process the dough. These devices fall under foodstuff equipment for making or processing dough and are classified in the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[A21C 7/06] combined with dividing machines with radial, i.e. star-like, cutter-blades slidable in the slots of, and relatively to, a pressing-plunger -- A21C 7/06 classifies dough-processing machines that combine a dividing unit with radial, star-like cutter blades that slide in the slots of, and relative to, a pressing plunger, enabling dough to be homogenised by means other than kneading. This subcategory falls under machines for making or processing dough within the foodstuff section of the CPC.
[A21C 9/00] Other apparatus for handling dough or dough pieces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Other apparatus for handling (opposed to shaping or forming) dough or dough pieces in the broadest sense not covered by groups A21C3/00 - A21C7/00 and A21C13/00 - A21C14/00 , such as:  Apparatus for conveying, positioning dough or dough pieces or dough carriers, e.g. tins or pans.  Apparatus for filling, e.g. by injection or folding, and apparatus for coating dough pieces, e.g. by brushing on a coating with a sweeping action, and laminating dough sheets.  Other apparatus like hand tools, dust hoods, cleaning arrangements and trays.
[A21C 9/02] Apparatus for hanging or distributing strings of dough, such as noodles, spaghetti or macaroni, on bars -- This subclass covers devices that suspend or feed strands of dough such as noodles, spaghetti or macaroni onto bars for drying or shaping. It includes apparatus designed to hang or distribute dough strings on supports during pasta production.
[A21C 9/04] Apparatus for spreading granular material on, or sweeping or coating the surfaces of, pieces or sheets of dough -- Apparatus for spreading granular material on dough pieces or sheets, or for sweeping or coating their surfaces, includes devices such as sprinklers, dusters, dusting rollers and similar machines used in bakeries to apply flour, sugar, seeds or other particulates.
[A21C 9/045] Laminating dough sheets combined with applying a filling in between, e.g. lasagna-type products  ravioli-making machines  A21C9/066 ; making laminated baked wafer sheets  A21C15/02 -- The A21C 9/045 class encompasses machines that laminate dough sheets while applying a filling between them, such as those used to produce layered foods like lasagna, ravioli, and similar products, and it also includes equipment for
[A21C 9/06] Apparatus for filling pieces of dough such as doughnuts -- The CPC subclass A21C 9/06 covers machines that fill individual dough pieces, such as doughnuts, after they have been shaped. It includes devices that inject fillings, apply glazes or toppings, and related mechanisms that handle dough or dough pieces during the final filling stage of production.
[A21C 9/061] Making continuous filled tubular products, e.g. with subsequent cutting -- A21C 9/061 relates to methods and apparatus for producing continuous filled tubular products from dough, such as extruded or rolled pastries that are filled during formation and later cut into portions. The entry covers the handling of dough or dough pieces to create a continuous tube with filling, and the subsequent cutting steps that define the final product size.
[A21C 9/063] Applying a folding, wrapping, rolling action  A21C9/045 ,  A21C9/061  take precedence -- A21C 9/063 covers machines or equipment that handle dough or dough pieces by applying a folding, wrapping or rolling action, such as devices that repeatedly fold and roll dough layers to create laminated products. It falls under the broader group for apparatus used in dough processing.
[A21C 9/065] by means of injection into a piece of dough  filling baked articles  A21C15/007 -- A21C 9/065 pertains to the method of injecting fillings into pieces of dough, specifically for baked goods. This classification is part of the broader category of machines or equipment used in food processing, focusing on specialized apparatus designed to handle and modify dough pieces before baking.
[A21C 9/066] using two separate sheets or pieces of dough with filling in between, e.g. crimped at their edges; Ravioli-making machines  A21C11/163  takes precedence -- The subclass includes machines that form filled dough items by joining two separate sheets or pieces of dough with a filling placed between them, sealing the edges together, as in equipment for making ravioli or similar products.
[A21C 9/068] Tools for crimping or trimming of edges or rims, e.g. hand-operated -- This subclass covers hand-operated devices used to crimp, trim or shape the edges or rims of dough pieces, such as pastry cutters, crust crimpers and similar tools employed in bakery equipment.
[A21C 9/08] Depositing, arranging and conveying apparatus for handling pieces, e.g. sheets of dough -- A21C 9/08 covers devices that deposit, arrange and transport dough pieces such as sheets during processing. It includes machinery that handles individual dough pieces in bakeries or food-production lines.
[A21C 9/081] Charging of baking tins or forms with dough -- A21C 9/081 covers equipment and methods for loading dough into baking tins or moulds, including devices that dispense, deposit or transfer dough into the desired shape before baking. It belongs to the subclass for other apparatus for handling dough or dough pieces within the foodstuffs hierarchy of the CPC.
[A21C 9/083] Manipulating tins, pans etc., e.g. charging or discharging conveyors, trolleys or ovens -- A21C 9/083 covers apparatus for handling dough by moving or positioning tins, pans and similar containers, such as conveyors, trolleys or ovens used to charge or discharge these items during dough-related processes.
[A21C 9/085] Separating, spacing, orienting or aligning discrete dough pieces, e.g. after passing a cutting device -- The A21C 9/085 subclass covers machinery that separates, spaces, or aligns individual dough pieces after they have been cut. It includes devices that orient or position discrete pieces of dough for further processing, ensuring consistent spacing and arrangement before baking or shaping.
[A21C 9/086] Stacking or unstacking discrete dough sheets  with filling in between sheets  A21C9/045 -- A21C 9/086 pertains to machinery designed for stacking or unstacking discrete dough sheets, particularly when a filling is placed between the sheets, as part of the broader category of apparatus for handling dough or dough pieces. This classification is under the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[A21C 9/088] Folding or bending discrete dough pieces or dough strips  twisting strips of dough  A21C3/08 ; with filling action  A21C9/063 ; forming toroid-shaped dough pieces  A21C11/002 -- The A21C 9/088 subclass covers equipment that folds, bends or twists discrete pieces or strips of dough, often incorporating a filling step and creating toroid-shaped portions. It falls under machines for making or processing dough, focusing on handling dough pieces rather than continuous sheets, and includes mechanisms that shape dough into rings or loops for baked products.
[A21D] TREATMENT OF FLOUR OR DOUGH FOR BAKING, e.g. BY ADDITION OF MATERIALS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Preservation of flour or dough before baking -> A23B40/00 - Preservation of bakery products -> A23B45/00
[A21D 10/00] Batters, dough or mixtures before baking -- A21D 10/00 relates to preparations such as batters, doughs or other mixtures prior to baking, covering the treatment of flour or dough by adding ingredients or processing steps to achieve desired baking properties.
[A21D 10/002] Dough mixes; Baking or bread improvers; Premixes -- A21D 10/002 covers compositions used to prepare batters, doughs or similar mixtures before baking, including premixed flour blends, dough mixes and bread-improving agents that are added to the base ingredients to enhance dough development, texture, rise or shelf-life. It pertains to formulations that combine flour with additional substances such as enzymes, emulsifiers, improvers or other functional additives intended for the treatment of flour or dough in baking processes.
[A21D 10/005] Solid, dry or compact materials; Granules; Powders -- This subgroup covers solid, dry or compact additives such as granules and powders that are mixed into flour or dough before baking, including leavening agents, enzymes, vitamins, minerals, flavorings and other functional powders.
[A21D 10/007] Liquids or pumpable materials -- A21D 10/007 relates to the incorporation of liquids or other pumpable substances into batters, doughs or similar mixtures before baking. It covers the selection, preparation and addition of fluids such as water, milk, oils, syrups or emulsions that modify dough properties, improve handling or affect the final baked product.
[A21D 10/02] Ready-for-oven doughs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ready-for-oven doughs
[A21D 10/025] Packaged doughs  packaging bakery products  B65B ,  B65D -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Special packaging features -> B65B , B65D
[A21D 10/04] Batters -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Batter for coating fish, meat, vegetables before frying -> A23P10/10
[A21D 10/045] Packaged batters  packaging bakery products  B65B ,  B65D -- A21D 10/045 relates to the preparation and packaging of batters, doughs or similar mixtures before baking, covering methods and containers for storing and transporting these bakery products. It falls under the human necessities hierarchy for foodstuffs and the treatment of flour or dough.
[A21D 13/00] Finished or partly finished bakery products -- This class pertains to bakery items that are already formed or only partially processed, such as breads, rolls, cakes, biscuits, pastries and similar products that are ready for consumption or for a final finishing step. It falls under the treatment of flour or dough for baking.
[A21D 13/02] Products made from whole meal -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bakery products characterised by the addition of whole meal or bran
[A21D 13/04] Products made from materials other than rye or wheat flour -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bakery products characterised by the addition of a larger quantity of other flour (rice flour, lupin flour, etc...) than wheat or rye flour
[A21D 13/043] from tubers, e.g. manioc or potato -- A21D 13/043 covers bakery items that are produced from tuber-derived starches or flours, such as those obtained from manioc or potato, and applied to finished or partially finished bakery products. It is part of the treatment of flour or dough for baking within the broader foodstuffs category of human necessities.
[A21D 13/045] from leguminous plants -- A21D 13/045 covers bakery products or dough treatments where the added material originates from leguminous plants such as beans, peas or lentils. It includes the use of legume-derived flours, proteins or extracts to improve texture, nutritional value or shelf-life of finished or partially finished breads, pastries or similar items.
[A21D 13/047] from cereals other than rye or wheat, e.g. rice -- A21D 13/047 classifies finished or partially finished bakery products that are prepared from cereals other than rye or wheat, such as rice, covering their specific formulation and processing.
[A21D 13/06] Products with modified nutritive value, e.g. with modified starch content -- A21D 13/06 covers finished or partially finished bakery products whose nutritional value has been altered, such as items with adjusted starch content to change caloric or dietary properties. The subclass includes breads, pastries and similar goods where ingredients are modified to provide a specific nutritional profile.
[A21D 13/062] with modified sugar content -- This subclass covers finished or partially finished bakery items where the proportion or type of sugar has been altered, such as reduced, increased, or substituted sugars, to affect sweetness, texture, or shelf life. It includes methods of adjusting sugar content in breads, cakes, pastries and
[A21D 13/064] with modified protein content -- A21D 13/064 pertains to finished or partly finished bakery products whose protein content has been intentionally altered, for example by adding protein-adjusting ingredients or by processing steps that increase or decrease protein levels, and is situated under the treatment of flour or dough for baking.
[A21D 13/066] Gluten-free products -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Gluten-free or gluten-less products  Bakery products for coeliac patients
[A21D 13/068] with modified fat content -- A21D 13/068 designates finished or partially finished bakery products whose fat content has been deliberately altered, such as reduced, enriched or replaced, to modify texture, flavor, shelf-life or nutritional profile. The class sits within the CPC hierarchy for treatment of flour or dough for baking, under foodstuffs and human necessities.
[A21D 13/10] Multi-layered products -- A21D 13/10 covers bakery items made of two or more distinct layers that are formed separately or assembled after baking, such as layered cakes, filled pastries and multi-tiered breads. It applies to finished or partially finished products where the layers may differ in composition, texture or flavor.
[A21D 13/11] made of two or more doughs, e.g. differing in composition, colour or structure -- A21D 13/11 relates to finished or partly finished bakery products that are produced from two or more doughs having different composition, colour or structure, such as layered, marbled or otherwise combined dough items.
[A21D 13/13] with coatings -- A21D 13/13 covers bakery items that receive an edible coating after baking, such as glazes, icings, sugar shells or other surface layers applied to finished or partially finished products like breads, pastries, biscuits or cakes.
[A21D 13/14] with fillings -- The A21D 13/14 subclass covers bakery products that contain fillings, such as pastries, filled breads, pies or buns, where the filling is incorporated during or after baking. It falls under the treatment of flour or dough for baking, specifically finished or partly finished bakery items with added interior or surface fillings.
[A21D 13/16] Multi-layered pastry, e.g. puff pastry -- A21D 13/16 covers multi-layered pastries such as puff pastry, where a dough sheet is repeatedly folded and rolled with a layer of fat to create numerous thin, alternating layers that rise and separate during baking. The class includes the structure, preparation methods and specific characteristics of these laminated bakery products.
[A21D 13/17] with coatings -- A21D 13/17 pertains to bakery items that receive a coating, such as glazes, icings, sugar shells or other surface layers applied after baking, intended to modify appearance, texture, flavor or shelf life. It is located under the treatment of flour or dough for baking within foodstuffs.
[A21D 13/19] with fillings -- A21D 13/19 pertains to finished or partly finished bakery products that incorporate a filling, such as pies, pastries, turnovers, donuts or filled buns. The classification covers the inclusion of any edible material placed inside the dough or batter, including creams, jams, meats, cheeses or vegetable mixtures, regardless of the method of insertion.
[A21D 13/20] Partially or completely coated products -- A21D 13/20 covers bakery items on which a surface layer is applied, either partially or completely, including glazes, icings, chocolate shells, sugar films or other edible coatings. The coating may be added before or after baking and can protect, flavor or decorate the product.
[A21D 13/22] coated before baking -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Products coated before baking or pre-baking
[A21D 13/24] coated after baking -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Products coated after baking or pre-baking
[A21D 13/26] the coating forming a barrier against migration -- A21D 13/26 covers coatings applied to finished or partially finished bakery products that act as a barrier to prevent migration of moisture, fats, flavours or other substances into or out of the item, such as edible films, glazes or icings designed to protect quality and extend shelf life.
[A21D 13/28] characterised by the coating composition -- A21D 13/28 relates to finished or partially finished bakery products whose defining feature is the specific coating composition applied, such as glazes, icings, or edible films, used to modify appearance, texture, or shelf-life. This subclass falls under treatment of flour or dough for baking within the broader foodstuffs category.
[A21D 13/30] Filled, to be filled or stuffed products -- The code A21D 13/30 covers bakery items that are filled, intended to be filled, or stuffed, such as pies, pastries, turnovers, and similar products where a filling is inserted into a dough or crust either before or after baking. It falls under the treatment of flour or dough for baking, specifically addressing finished or partly finished bakery goods with interior fillings.
[A21D 13/31] filled before baking -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Products filled or stuffed before baking or pre-baking
[A21D 13/32] filled or to be filled after baking, e.g. sandwiches -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Products filled or stuffed after baking or pre-baking
[A21D 13/33] Edible containers, e.g. cups or cones -- A21D 13/33 classifies bakery-related edible containers such as cups or cones that are formed from dough or other bakery materials and intended to hold food or liquids. The entry covers finished or partially finished products designed for serving, including the methods of shaping or reinforcing them for functional use.
[A21D 13/34] the filling forming a barrier against migration -- A21D 13/34 relates to bakery items in which the filling is designed to act as a barrier that prevents migration of moisture, oil, flavour or other components between the filling and the surrounding dough or crust, thereby improving product stability, texture and shelf-life.
[A21D 13/36] Filled wafers -- A21D 13/36 relates to filled wafers, describing bakery products in which a baked wafer base is combined with a filling such as cream, jam, chocolate or similar substances, resulting in a finished or partially finished confection.
[A21D 13/37] Co-extruded products, i.e. products obtained by simultaneous extrusion of the dough and the filling -- This subclass covers bakery items produced by co-extrusion, where dough and a filling are extruded together in a single step to form a finished or partially finished product. It pertains to the processing of flour or dough for baked goods and includes both sweet and savoury filled items.
[A21D 13/38] characterised by the filling composition -- A21D 13/38 is a CPC subclass for finished or partly finished bakery products whose defining feature is the composition of the filling, covering items such as pies, pastries or filled breads where the specific ingredients or formulation of the interior layer are the primary technical characteristic.
[A21D 13/40] Products characterised by the type, form or use -- This CPC subclass A21D 13/40 covers bakery items that are distinguished by their specific type, shape or intended application, such as particular kinds of breads, pastries or confectionery. It falls under treatments of flour or dough for baking and includes finished or partially finished products.
[A21D 13/41] Pizzas -- The A21D 13/41 entry covers pizzas, i.e., prepared bakery products consisting of a dough base that may be partially or fully baked and is combined with sauces, cheeses, meats, vegetables or other toppings, and is intended for consumption as a finished food item. It includes methods of preparing, shaping, topping and cooking the pizza, and related apparatuses.
[A21D 13/42] Tortillas -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Soft tortillas and other flat breads (pita, chapati, etc.)
[A21D 13/43] Flatbreads, e.g. naan -- A21D 13/43 covers finished or partially finished flatbread products such as naan, falling under the treatment of flour or dough for baking within the broader foodstuff category. It includes processes and compositions specific to flat, leavened or unleavened breads.
[A21D 13/44] Pancakes or crêpes -- A21D 13/44 pertains to the formulation, treatment and cooking of pancake or crêpe batters and the resulting thin, flat bakery products, including variations in ingredients, leavening and preparation methods. It covers finished or partially finished items derived from flour or dough that have been processed for these specific types of flat cakes.
[A21D 13/45] Wafers -- A21D 13/45 pertains to wafers, encompassing finished or partially-finished bakery products obtained by treating flour or dough for baking, such as those formed from added ingredients or processing steps.
[A21D 13/46] Croutons -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Croutons made from bread or bakery products  Pieces of bread or bakery products
[A21D 13/47] Decorated or decorative products -- A21D 13/47 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers decorated or decorative bakery products, focusing on items where aesthetic enhancements are a key feature. This includes finished or partly finished goods that have been embellished for visual appeal.
[A21D 13/48] Products with an additional function other than for eating, e.g. toys or cutlery -- In CPC A21D 13/48 the focus is on bakery items that serve a purpose beyond eating, such as built-in toys, cutlery, or other functional features. It covers finished or partly finished products where the primary food component is combined with an additional non-nutritional role, extending the use of the bakery item to play, serving or handling.
[A21D 13/50] Solidified foamed products, e.g. meringues -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Foamed bakery products or pastry
[A21D 13/60] Deep-fried products, e.g. doughnuts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fried bakery products from a dough: doughnuts, beignets
[A21D 13/80] Pastry not otherwise provided for elsewhere, e.g. cakes, biscuits or cookies -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Biscuits, cookies, cake , chocolate cake (without special characteristics)

[A21D 17/00] Refreshing bakery products -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Refreshing or recycling baked or pre-baked bakery products.
[A21D 17/002] Recycling, e.g. for use in baking or for animal consumption -- A21D 17/002 covers recycling methods for flour, dough or baked-goods waste, including processes that recover and treat such material for reuse in further baking or for conversion into animal feed, within the broader category of foodstuff treatment.
[A21D 17/004] refreshing by thawing or heating -- This CPC subclass A21D 17/004 covers methods of refreshing bakery products, specifically by thawing or heating to restore texture and flavor after storage. It falls under treatment of flour or dough for baking, aimed at improving quality of ready-made baked goods.
[A21D 17/006] with microwaves -- A21D 17/006 covers bakery products whose preparation or post-baking treatment involves microwave energy, such as using microwaves to heat, crisp, or otherwise modify the texture, moisture or flavor of dough-based items. The code falls under the CPC section for the treatment of flour or dough for baking, specifically addressing microwave-based processes for refreshing or finishing baked goods.
