[A24] TOBACCO -- In this class, the following terms or expressions are used with the meaning indicated: " simulated smoking devices" means devices simulating the act of smoking without burning tobacco, e.g. so called electronic cigarettes; "cigarette" means: a small tube of wrapper containing tobacco or tobacco substitutes which people smoke by igniting it directly and where the tobacco (or the tobacco substitute) is burned; in a broader sense it also means a small tube of wrapper, containing tobacco or tobacco substitutes, which is associated with a heat source at manufacturing or inserted by the user in a device containing a heat source and where the tobacco (or the tobacco substitute) is heated without being burned. In this broader sense, the word "rod" often also is used in place of cigarette.
[A24B] MANUFACTURE OR PREPARATION OF TOBACCO FOR SMOKING OR CHEWING -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[A24B 1/00] Preparation of tobacco on the plantation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Preparation of harvested tobacco on the plantation.
[A24B 1/02] Arrangements in barns for preparatory treatment of the tobacco, e.g. with devices for drying -- The A24B 1/02 subclass covers installations inside barns that are used for preparatory treatment of tobacco on the plantation, such as systems and devices for drying the leaf before curing. It is part of the broader A24B group on manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 1/04] Sifting, sorting, cleaning or removing impurities from tobacco -- A24B 1/04 pertains to the processing steps in which tobacco harvested on a plantation is sifted, sorted, cleaned or otherwise freed of impurities before further manufacturing or preparation for smoking or chewing. It falls under the broader category of manufacturing or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 1/06] Stringing tobacco leaves -- This CPC entry covers the step of arranging harvested tobacco leaves onto strings or racks for drying and further processing on the plantation. It falls under human necessities, tobacco, manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing, specifically preparation on the plantation.
[A24B 1/08] Suspending devices for tobacco leaves -- The CPC subclass A24B 1/08 covers devices that hold or suspend tobacco leaves during processing, allowing them to dry, cure, or age without contact with surfaces. Such suspending systems are used on plantations to improve airflow and uniformity in leaf treatment, facilitating more consistent tobacco quality.
[A24B 1/10] Packing or pressing tobacco -- A24B 1/10 covers the techniques and equipment used to pack or press tobacco after it has been prepared on the plantation, including methods for compressing, forming, and containing tobacco for later use in smoking or chewing products. This subclass falls under the manufacture or preparation of tobacco within the human necessities field.
[A24B 11/00] Tobacco-twisting machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines for making twist or spun tobacco.
[A24B 13/00] Tobacco for pipes, for cigars, e.g. cigar inserts, or for cigarettes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Tobacco per se, for pipes, cigars or cigarettes.  Chewing tobacco  Snuff  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Reconstituted tobacco products -> A24B3/14
[A24B 13/02] Flakes or shreds of tobacco -- A24B 13/02 refers to the preparation of tobacco that has been cut into flakes or shredded pieces for use in smoking or chewing items. It is situated within the human necessities hierarchy under tobacco, specifically the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for pipes, cigars or cigarettes.
[A24B 15/00] Chemical features or treatment of tobacco -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Chemical features of tobacco, smoking or smokeless tobacco as well as reconstituted tobacco, including chemical treatments, modifying tobacco by chemical agents, e.g. flavourants. This main group also includes biochemical treatments of tobacco and physical treatments such as wave energy, irradiation and electrical treatments.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Preparing tobacco in the factory, e.g. steaming, drying, curing, puffing, thermal treatments -> A24B3/00  **Glossary:** - Organic solvent: Carbon containing solvent except carbon dioxide. - Tobacco substitute: This definition encompasses both non-tobacco plants suitable for smoking or as replacer for cigarette and substitute of cigarettes comprising tobacco extracts, e.g. flavours and possibly nicotine
[A24B 15/10] Chemical features of tobacco products or tobacco substitutes -- A24B 15/10 covers the chemical characteristics of tobacco products and their substitutes, including the composition of the plant material, added ingredients, flavourings, and any treatments that alter the chemical profile. It pertains to the formulation and modification of tobacco for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 15/12] of reconstituted tobacco -- This CPC subclass A24B 15/12 covers the chemical characteristics and processing methods applied to reconstituted tobacco, a material made from tobacco fibers
[A24B 15/14] made of tobacco and a binding agent not derived from tobacco -- A24B 15/14 classifies products that consist of tobacco combined with a binding agent that is not derived from tobacco, such as synthetic adhesives or other fillers, used in the manufacture of smoking or chewing tobacco items.
[A24B 15/16] of tobacco substitutes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Tobacco substitutes, this group contains both non-tobacco plants suitable for smoking or as replacer for cigarette and substitute of cigarettes comprising tobacco extracts, e.g. flavours and possibly nicotine.
[A24B 15/165] comprising as heat source a carbon fuel or an oxidized or thermally degraded carbonaceous fuel, e.g. carbohydrates, cellulosic material -- This subclass covers tobacco products in which the heating element uses carbon-based fuels such as charcoal, oxidized carbon, or thermally degraded organic
[A24B 15/167] in liquid or vaporisable form, e.g. liquid compositions for electronic cigarettes -- A24B 15/167 relates to the preparation of tobacco in liquid or vaporisable form, such as the liquid compositions used in electronic cigarettes, covering the formulation of fluids that generate an inhalable aerosol.
[A24B 15/18] Treatment of tobacco products or tobacco substitutes -- This subclass covers processes applied to finished tobacco items or alternative substances to modify their properties, such as flavor, moisture content, combustion characteristics, nicotine delivery or preservation, and includes chemical or physical treatments like aging, flavoring, additive incorporation, or moisture control.
[A24B 15/183] sterilization, preservation or biological decontamination -- The A24B 15/183 subclass concerns processes applied to tobacco during its manufacture or preparation that eliminate or control microorganisms, preserve product quality, or achieve biological decontamination. It includes sterilisation techniques, preservation treatments and other methods designed to keep tobacco free from harmful microbial contamination.
[A24B 15/186] by coating with a coating composition, encapsulation of tobacco particles  encapsulation of additives other than tobacco :  A24B15/283 -- A24B 15/186 pertains to the chemical treatment of tobacco for smoking or chewing, specifically involving the process of coating tobacco particles or encapsulating additives that are not tobacco. This method is used to modify the sensory properties or stability of the product.
[A24B 15/20] Biochemical treatment -- A24B 15/20 covers biochemical methods applied to tobacco, such as enzymatic, microbial or fermentation processes that modify the leaf's chemical composition before it is prepared for smoking or chewing. The entry includes treatments that alter nicotine, flavor precursors, or other constituents to improve product characteristics.
[A24B 15/22] by application of electric or wave energy or particle radiation -- A24B 15/22 pertains to the chemical treatment of tobacco for smoking or chewing, specifically focusing on methods that involve the application of electric or wave energy, or particle radiation. This classification is part of a broader hierarchy under human necessities and tobacco manufacturing processes.
[A24B 15/24] by extraction -- A24B 15/24 covers chemical treatment of tobacco that involves extracting constituents from the plant material. It includes methods such as solvent or mechanical extraction to isolate nicotine, flavors or other substances for use in smoking or chewing products.
[A24B 15/241] Extraction of specific substances -- A24B 15/241 covers methods for extracting particular chemical constituents from tobacco, including isolation of nicotine
[A24B 15/243] Nicotine -- A24B 15/243 pertains to nicotine, the main alkaloid present in tobacco, and addresses its chemical properties, synthesis, isolation, and application in the manufacture or processing of tobacco products for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 15/245] Nitrosamines -- A24B 15/245 covers the presence, formation or reduction of nitrosamines in tobacco
[A24B 15/246] Polycyclic aromatic compounds -- A24B 15/246 covers polycyclic aromatic compounds that are present in or applied to tobacco, including additives, flavoring agents and contaminants formed during processing or smoking, as well as methods for their detection, removal or modification in tobacco products.
[A24B 15/248] Heavy metals -- The subclass A24B 15/248 covers chemical methods and compositions used to remove, reduce, or control heavy-metal contaminants in tobacco products, including detection, treatment, and processing steps that limit the presence of metals such as lead, cadmium or arsenic during manufacture or preparation.
[A24B 15/26] Use of organic solvents for extraction -- The CPC subclass A24B 15/26 concerns the use of organic solvents to extract nicotine, flavors or other constituents from tobacco during its manufacture or preparation for smoking or chewing. It falls under chemical processing methods applied to tobacco products.
[A24B 15/28] by chemical substances -- In groups A24B15/30 - A24B15/42 , the last place priority rule is applied, i.e. at each hierarchical level, in the absence of an indication to the contrary, classification is made in the last appropriate place for a substance. This Note corresponds to IPC Note (1) relating to A24B15/30 - A24B15/42 .
[A24B 15/281] the action of the chemical substances being delayed -- A24B 15/281 relates to tobacco manufacturing processes in which chemical substances are deliberately delayed in their action, such as retardants that slow nicotine release, flavor development or other additive effects during preparation of smoking or chewing tobacco. The subclass covers methods and compositions designed to control the timing of these chemical reactions.
[A24B 15/282] by indirect addition of the chemical substances, e.g. in the wrapper, in the case -- This subclass covers methods that introduce chemical substances into tobacco products indirectly, for example by applying them to the wrapper or paper rather than mixing them directly with the tobacco. It pertains to the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 15/283] by encapsulation of the chemical substances -- A24B 15/283 covers methods of preparing tobacco for smoking or chewing in which chemical substances are encapsulated within the tobacco material. The classification addresses techniques that isolate additives-such as flavors, nicotine, or other agents-inside micro-capsules or similar carriers to control their release during use. This falls under the broader group dealing with chemical treatment of tobacco.
[A24B 15/284] the additive being bound to a host by chemical, electrical or like forces, e.g. use of precursors, inclusion complexes -- A24B 15/284 covers methods for attaching additives to a tobacco host through chemical, electrical or similar forces, such as using precursor compounds or forming inclusion complexes, to modify the tobacco's properties. This subclass falls under the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 15/285] characterised by structural features, e.g. particle shape or size -- A24B 15/285 pertains to tobacco preparations whose defining aspect is a structural characteristic such as particle shape, size, or morphology, rather than a specific chemical composition. It covers methods, additives or processes that create or modify these physical features to influence the behaviour of smoking or chewing tobacco.
[A24B 15/286] Nanoparticles -- A24B 15/286 covers the use of nanoparticles in the chemical processing or treatment of tobacco, such as modifying flavor, reducing harmful constituents, or enhancing delivery in smoking or chewing products. It belongs to the CPC group for manufacture or preparation of tobacco for consumption.
[A24B 15/287] by inorganic substances only -- A24B 15/287 relates to processes that treat tobacco using only inorganic substances, such as salts, metals, oxides or other non-organic compounds, to modify properties like flavor, combustibility or preservation during the manufacture of smoking or chewing tobacco. This sub-class falls under chemical features or treatment of tobacco.
[A24B 15/288] Catalysts or catalytic material, e.g. included in the wrapping material -- A24B 15/288 covers the use of catalysts or catalytic substances incorporated into the wrapping material of tobacco products. It relates to treating tobacco during manufacture by embedding catalytic agents in the paper, foil or similar wrapper to modify combustion, flavor or reduce harmful emissions.
[A24B 15/30] by organic substances -- A24B 15/30 covers the chemical treatment of tobacco using organic substances, detailing processes that modify or enhance tobacco for smoking or chewing. It includes the application of natural or synthetic organic compounds during manufacture or preparation.
[A24B 15/301] by aromatic compounds -- A24B 15/301 concerns the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing in which aromatic compounds are employed, such as flavoring agents or additives that modify the tobacco's scent, taste or chemical properties during processing. This sub-class addresses the use of aromatic chemicals to treat tobacco products.
[A24B 15/302] by natural substances obtained from animals or plants -- A24B 15/302 relates to processes that treat tobacco with natural substances obtained from animals or plants, such as extracts, essential oils or enzymes, to alter flavor, aroma or other properties of smoking or chewing tobacco. It falls under the CPC hierarchy Human Necessities - Tobacco - Manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing - Chemical features or treatment of tobacco.
[A24B 15/303] Plant extracts other than tobacco -- A24B 15/303 concerns the incorporation of plant extracts that are not derived from tobacco into the chemical treatment of tobacco. It covers methods and compositions in which such extracts are added during manufacturing to alter flavor, aroma, or other properties of smoking or chewing tobacco.
[A24B 15/305] of undetermined constitution characterised by their preparation -- A24B 15/305 classifies tobacco items whose exact chemical makeup is unspecified but are identified by the way they are prepared, covering products distinguished only by their processing steps within the manufacture or preparation of smoking or chewing tobacco.
[A24B 15/306] one reactant being an amino acid or a protein, e.g. Maillard's reaction  A24B15/307  takes precedence -- A24B 15/306 covers tobacco processing steps in which an amino acid or protein acts as a reactant, such as the Maillard reaction that creates flavor and colour compounds. It is a sub-category of chemical treatments of tobacco, but is superseded by the more specific A24B 15/307.
[A24B 15/307] using microorganisms or enzymes as catalysts -- This classification covers the use of microorganisms or enzymes to catalyze chemical processes in the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing, such as fermentation, biotransformation or enzymatic treatment to modify flavor, nicotine content or other constituents.
[A24B 15/308] vitamins -- The A24B 15/308 subclass deals with the incorporation of vitamins into tobacco products, covering methods for adding, formulating or treating smoking or chewing tobacco with vitamin substances to alter nutritional or functional properties. It includes processes, compositions and additives that feature vitamins as active components.
[A24B 15/32] by acyclic compounds -- A24B 15/32 covers methods and processes for treating tobacco with acyclic compounds during its manufacture or preparation for smoking or chewing. The classification includes the use of non-cyclic organic substances to modify flavour, nicotine content or other properties of the tobacco.
[A24B 15/34] containing a carbocyclic ring other than a six-membered aromatic ring -- A24B 15/34 classifies tobacco manufacturing or preparation steps that incorporate additives or treatments containing a carbocyclic ring other than a six-membered aromatic ring, such as cyclobutane or cyclopentane structures, used in products for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 15/345] containing condensed rings -- A24B 15/345 covers chemical modifications of tobacco in which the additives or treatments contain condensed ring structures. It pertains to the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing where such ring
[A24B 15/36] containing a heterocyclic ring -- A24B 15/36 covers tobacco articles that incorporate a heterocyclic ring within their composition, focusing on the chemical structure or treatment of the tobacco. It falls under the broader categories of human necessities, tobacco, and the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 15/365] having nitrogen and sulfur as hetero atoms in the same ring -- A24B 15/365 covers tobacco manufacturing or treatment processes that create heterocyclic compounds in which nitrogen and sulfur appear together as hetero-atoms within the same ring, typically to alter flavor, nicotine release or combustion properties.
[A24B 15/38] having only nitrogen as hetero atom -- A24B 15/38 designates tobacco treatments in which the only hetero-atom present in the modifying compound is nitrogen. It covers processes or additives that alter tobacco's chemical properties employing nitrogen-based molecules, without incorporating other hetero-atoms such as oxygen or sulfur.
[A24B 15/385] in a five-membered ring -- A24B 15/385 pertains to the chemical treatment of tobacco, specifically focusing on modifications involving compounds with a five-membered ring structure. This classification is part of the broader category dealing with the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 15/40] having only oxygen or sulfur as hetero atoms -- A24B 15/40 is a CPC subgroup under Tobacco → Manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing that deals with chemical features or treatments in which the tobacco-related compounds contain only oxygen or sulfur as heteroatoms, covering processes such as oxidation, sulfonation and related modifications.
[A24B 15/403] having only oxygen as hetero atoms -- A24B 15/403 covers tobacco manufacturing or preparation methods that involve chemical modifications in which oxygen is the sole hetero atom present. It pertains to treatments of tobacco where no other hetero elements such as nitrogen, sulfur or halogens are introduced, focusing on oxygen-based reactions.
[A24B 15/406] in a five-membered ring -- A24B 15/406 covers tobacco treatments that involve incorporating a compound into a five-membered ring structure, such as pyrrolidine or imidazole derivatives, during the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing. This subgroup specifies the chemical modification of tobacco by agents featuring a five-membered heterocycle.
[A24B 15/42] by organic and inorganic substances -- A24B 15/42 pertains to the chemical treatment of tobacco for smoking or chewing, specifically involving the use of organic and inorganic substances. This classification is part of a broader hierarchy that addresses human necessities, including the manufacture and preparation of tobacco products.
[A24B 3/00] Preparing tobacco in the factory -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Primary treatment and preparation of the tobacco in the factory.
[A24B 3/02] Humidifying packed raw tobacco -- A24B 3/02 covers processes and devices that add moisture to already packed raw tobacco to keep it supple and prevent drying after packaging. It includes controlled humidification techniques applied in factories to preserve tobacco quality before further processing or sale.
[A24B 3/04] Humidifying or drying tobacco bunches or cut tobacco -- A24B 3/04 covers processes used in the manufacture of tobacco that adjust moisture levels, specifically methods for humidifying or drying tobacco bunches or cut tobacco to achieve desired properties for smoking or chewing products.
[A24B 3/06] Loosening tobacco leaves or cut tobacco -- A24B 3/06 addresses methods and apparatuses for loosening tobacco leaves or cut tobacco during factory processing, including techniques that separate, aerate or increase the volume of the material to improve handling and subsequent manufacturing steps.
[A24B 3/07] Cutting or removing tie leaves -- A24B 3/07 relates to the step of cutting or removing the tie leaves that hold a bunch of tobacco together, usually after the leaves have been assembled and before further processing or packaging. This operation improves the uniformity and quality of the final tobacco product.
[A24B 3/08] Blending tobacco -- The A24B 3/08 entry covers methods for mixing different tobacco varieties or additives to produce a uniform blend intended for cigarettes, cigars, pipe tobacco or chewing tobacco. It includes selecting component tobaccos, determining proportions, and homogenising the mixture during factory preparation. This classification falls under Human Necessities → Tobacco → Manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing → Preparing tobacco in the factory.
[A24B 3/10] Roasting or cooling tobacco -- A24B 3/10 covers methods of heating or lowering the temperature of tobacco during its factory processing, such as roasting, baking, steaming, or cooling, to modify flavor, moisture content, or chemical composition before the product is packaged for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 3/12] Steaming, curing, or flavouring tobacco -- The subclass A24B 3/12 covers processes applied to tobacco in the factory that involve steaming, curing, or adding flavourings. It includes methods for treating tobacco leaves to develop desired moisture, aroma, and taste before they are used for smoking or chewing. This entry falls under the hierarchy of human necessities, tobacco, and the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 3/14] Forming reconstituted tobacco products, e.g. wrapper materials, sheets, imitation leaves, rods, cakes -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Delustering -> A24C1/40
[A24B 3/16] Classifying or aligning leaves -- A24B 3/16 covers the steps of classifying and aligning tobacco leaves during manufacturing, such as sorting, grading and arranging leaves to achieve uniform size, quality or orientation before further processing or curing. This sub-group relates to the preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing, focusing on leaf handling techniques that improve product consistency.
[A24B 3/18] Other treatment of leaves, e.g. puffing, crimpling, cleaning -- A24B 3/18 covers auxiliary processes applied to tobacco leaves after harvesting, such as puffing to expand the leaf surface, crimpling to create folds, and cleaning to remove impurities, all aimed at improving the quality and handling of tobacco before further manufacturing steps. This falls under the broader category of preparing tobacco for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 3/182] Puffing -- **Glossary:** - Puffing: expanding tobacco
[A24B 3/185] by impregnating with a liquid and subsequently freezing and evaporating this liquid -- A24B 3/185 covers a tobacco preparation method where the material is first impregnated with a liquid, then the impregnated tobacco is frozen and the liquid is subsequently evaporated, resulting in a modified product with altered moisture or flavor characteristics. This technique is used in the manufacture of smoking or chewing tobacco.
[A24B 3/187] by electrical treatment -- A24B 3/187 covers the use of electrical methods during the factory preparation of tobacco, such as applying electric currents, radio-frequency heating, ionisation or electrostatic treatment to dry, cure, flavour or sterilise the material before it is formed into smoking or chewing products.
[A24B 5/00] Stripping tobacco -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Separation of the tobacco lamina from the stem or rib.  **Glossary:** - Stem: the main vein of the tobacco leaf - Leaf part or lamina: the leaf blade, leaf tissue that has been separated from the midrib or stem
[A24B 5/02] by plucking out the stem -- A24B 5/02 covers the removal of tobacco stems by manually plucking them out of the leaf, a step in preparing tobacco for smoking or chewing. This method separates stems without cutting or crushing, allowing cleaner subsequent processes such as curing, slicing or blending.
[A24B 5/04] by cutting out the stem -- A24B 5/04 covers methods for stripping tobacco in which the stem is removed by cutting it away from the leaf. This subclass falls under the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing and concerns the mechanical separation of the stem from the tobacco material.
[A24B 5/06] by stripping leaf-parts from the stem -- A24B 5/06 covers the step of stripping leaf-parts from the stem during the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing, a subcategory of processes that prepare tobacco products.
[A24B 5/08] by cutting-off, shaving off, pressing flat the thick parts of stems or ribs -- A24B 5/08 pertains to tobacco manufacturing processes where the thick stem or rib sections are stripped by cutting them off, shaving them away, or pressing them flat. This classification falls under the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Tobacco → Manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 5/10] by crushing the leaves with subsequent separating -- The subclass A24B 5/10 describes a tobacco-preparation method in which the leaves are first crushed and then the resulting material is separated, a step used to facilitate later processing such as cutting, mixing or blending for cigarettes or chewing tobacco.
[A24B 5/12] Auxiliary devices for stripping -- The A24B 5/12 subclass covers auxiliary devices employed in the stripping of tobacco, that is, tools or mechanisms that assist in removing stems, veins or other unwanted material from tobacco leaves during preparation for smoking or chewing. It falls under the CPC hierarchy Human necessities → Tobacco → Manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing → Stripping tobacco.
[A24B 5/14] Flattening machines for leaves or stems -- A24B 5/14 covers machines that flatten tobacco leaves or stems, a step in preparing tobacco for smoking or chewing. These devices are used to press and even out the material before further processing such as cutting, blending or curing.
[A24B 5/16] Other treatment of stems or ribs, e.g. bending, chopping, incising -- A24B 5/16 covers secondary processing of tobacco stems or ribs after stripping, including mechanical actions such as bending, chopping or incising to alter their shape or texture. These treatments are applied to improve handling, blending or combustion characteristics of the tobacco material.
[A24B 7/00] Cutting tobacco -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cutting tobacco material, e.g. for forming the filler of a cigarette rod.
[A24B 7/02] by machines with reciprocating knives -- A24B 7/02 covers the method of cutting tobacco using machines equipped with reciprocating knives. It belongs to the CPC group for manufacturing or preparing tobacco for smoking or chewing, under the broader human necessities category.
[A24B 7/04] by machines with revolving knives -- A24B 7/04 covers the cutting of tobacco by machines that use revolving knives. The subclass specifies equipment and processes where a rotating blade array slices tobacco leaves or shreds for smoking or chewing products, distinguishing it from other cutting methods such as straight blades or hand-operated tools. This falls under the broader CPC group for manufacture or preparation of tobacco.
[A24B 7/06] with two co-operating sets of knife discs -- A24B 7/06 refers to a cutting-tobacco device that uses two cooperating sets of knife discs to shear and size tobacco leaves or strands. The twin disc arrangement provides a continuous, uniform cut, improving preparation efficiency for smoking or chewing products. This subclass falls under the CPC hierarchy for tobacco manufacturing and preparation.
[A24B 7/08] with several knives which act one after the other -- A24B 7/08 relates to tobacco-cutting apparatus that uses a series of knives arranged to act one after another, each successive blade further reducing the size of the tobacco. The sequential knife configuration provides a more uniform and efficient cut for smoking or chewing preparations.
[A24B 7/10] with cutter axes parallel to the feeding direction -- A24B 7/10 covers devices for cutting tobacco where the cutter axes are arranged parallel to the feeding direction of the material. It belongs to the Human Necessities group, under Tobacco and the manufacture or preparation of tobacco for smoking or chewing.
[A24B 7/12] with cutter axes transverse to the feeding direction -- This subclass covers tobacco cutting devices in which the cutting blades are oriented across the material's path. It includes mechanisms where the cutter axes run perpendicular to the feeding direction, typical of slicers or shredders used in manufacturing cigarettes
[A24B 7/14] Feeding or control devices for tobacco-cutting apparatus -- A24B 7/14 pertains to feeding or control devices for tobacco-cutting apparatus, encompassing mechanisms that supply tobacco to the cutter or regulate the cutting action. The code covers equipment that manages the flow of tobacco into the cutting system or adjusts cutting parameters for smoking or chewing products.
[A24B 9/00] Control of the moisture content of tobacco products, e.g. cigars, cigarettes, pipe tobacco -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control of the moisture content of the tobacco products (in the factory).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for use by the smokers for controlling the moisture content of tobacco products -> A24F25/00
[A24C] MACHINES FOR MAKING CIGARS OR CIGARETTES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machine and methods for making cigars or cigarettes, including making tipping materials for cigarettes and cigars and attaching filters to cigarette or cigars.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Packaging cigarettes -> B65B19/02  **Glossary:** - Cigarette: "cigarette" means: (1) a small tube of wrapper containing tobacco or tobacco substitutes which people smoke by igniting it directly and where the tobacco (or the tobacco substitute) is burned; (2) in a broader sense it also means a small tube of wrapper containing tobacco or tobacco substitutes which is associated with a heat source at manufacturing or inserted by the user in a device containing a heat source, and where the tobacco (or the tobacco substitute) is heated without being burned. In this broader sense, the word "rod" is also often used in place of cigarette. - Simulated smoking device: "simulated smoking devices" means devices simulating the act of smoking without burning tobacco, e.g. so called electronic cigarettes
[A24C 1/00] Making cigars -- **Definition:** This place covers:  A particular element, device or step of a method for the production of cigars.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Complete manufacture of cigars -> A24C3/00 - Cigars as products per se -> A24D1/00 - Bands for cigars -> A24D1/10

This sub-group falls under the broader category of machines for making cigars or cigarettes within the Human Necessities sector.

This treatment is used after cigar formation to enhance grip and aesthetic consistency.

These machines are used in the cigar-making process to increase efficiency and product consistency.

[A24C 5/007] Perforating -- A24C 5/007 covers techniques and devices for creating perforations in cigarette components during manufacturing. The perforations may be made in the tobacco rod, filter or paper wrapper to modify draw resistance, ventilation and smoke dilution.

These machines operate without intermittent stops, feeding tobacco and paper in a steady flow to produce long rods that are subsequently sliced. The technology enables high-speed, automated production of uniform cigarettes.

[A24C 5/1871] Devices for regulating the tobacco quantity -- A24C 5/1871 covers mechanisms that control the amount of tobacco delivered to cigarette-making equipment, such as variable dosers, metering rollers or adjustable feed channels that ensure consistent tobacco weight per product. [A24C 5/1878] for oval cigarettes -- A24C 5/1878 covers devices or methods specifically designed to produce oval-shaped cigarettes. It is a subclass of machines for making cigars or cigarettes within the human necessities and tobacco sections.
[A24C 5/1885] for cigarettes with an axial air duct -- A24C 5/1885 refers to cigarette-making machines that produce cigarettes incorporating an axial air duct running through the tobacco column. The duct provides a controlled air channel from the filter end toward the burning zone, improving draw resistance and delivering a steadier flow of air to the smoker.
[A24C 5/1892] with additives, e.g. binding agent, flavorants -- This CPC subclass covers cigarette-making machines that incorporate additives such as binding agents or flavorants into the tobacco product. It relates to apparatus that blend or apply these substances during the formation of cigarettes.

This improves seam integrity and overall product consistency in automated smoking-product manufacturing.

[A24C 5/26] Drying the seam -- A24C 5/26 - Drying the seam covers the step of removing moisture from the stitched joint of a cigarette or cigar after assembly, usually by applying heated air, infrared or other drying methods to ensure a properly sealed product before packaging.
[A24C 5/28] Cutting-off the tobacco rod -- A24C 5/28 covers the step in cigarette-making machines where the formed tobacco rod is severed from the production line. [A24C 5/31] with special arrangements coming into operation during starting, slowing-down or breakdown of the machine, e.g. for diverting or breaking the continuous rod -- The subclass A24C 5/31 covers devices in cigarette-making machines that are triggered during start-up, deceleration or failure, such as mechanisms that divert or break the continuous tobacco rod to manage interruptions in production.
