[A24F 3/00] Tobacco pipes combined with other objects -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Tobacco pipes combined with other objects, e.g. with cleaning appliances.
[A24F 3/02] with cleaning appliances -- A24F 3/02 classifies tobacco pipes that are integrated with cleaning appliances, covering designs that incorporate built-in brushes, scraper mechanisms or other tools for removing residue from the pipe during or after use.
[A24F 31/00] Pipe-spills -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Holders for spills, tapers or spent matches.
[A24F 40/00] Simulated smoking devices -- The CPC entry A24F 40/00 covers simulated smoking devices, products that mimic the act of smoking without containing tobacco, such as electronic cigarettes, herbal vaporizers or other nicotine-free alternatives. These items are classified under Human Necessities - Tobacco - Smokers' Requisites.
[A24F 40/05] Devices without heating means -- A24F 40/05 covers simulated-smoking devices that create a smoking-like experience without using a heating element. The class includes systems that generate nicotine or flavored aerosol through chemical reactions, pressurised delivery, or other non-thermal means, thereby imitating smoking while avoiding combustion or heat.
[A24F 40/10] Devices using liquid inhalable precursors -- A24F 40/10 covers devices that generate an inhalable aerosol or vapor from a liquid precursor, intended as simulated smoking devices. These include e-cig
[A24F 40/20] Devices using solid inhalable precursors -- Devices that generate aerosol or vapor from solid, inhalable precursors, such as heated tobacco sticks, nicotine tablets, or herbal granules, and are intended for use in simulated smoking products. The system typically incorporates a heating element that activates the solid material without combustion, delivering a nicotine-containing or flavored aerosol to the user. This classification covers all solid-precursor delivery mechanisms within simulated smoking devices.
[A24F 40/30] Devices using two or more structurally separated inhalable precursors, e.g. using two liquid precursors in two cartridges -- Devices that contain two or more physically separated inhalable precursors, such as dual-cartridge e-cigarettes, which store distinct liquid formulations in separate containers and combine them during the inhalation process.
[A24F 40/40] Constructional details, e.g. connection of cartridges and battery parts -- A24F 40/40 covers the structural design of simulated smoking devices, specifically detailing how cartridges are linked to battery components, including fastening methods, connectors, and integration features that ensure secure assembly and reliable power transfer.
[A24F 40/42] Cartridges or containers for inhalable precursors -- A24F 40/42 covers cartridges or containers that hold inhalable precursors for simulated smoking devices such as e-cigarettes. It includes designs for storage, refillable or disposable units that deliver liquids, gels or powders intended for vaporisation, and addresses features that protect the contents and enable controlled release during use. This falls under Human Necessities → Tobacco → Smokers' Requisites → Simulated Smoking Devices.
[A24F 40/44] Wicks -- A24F 40/44 covers wicks employed in simulated smoking devices, such as e-cigarettes or heat-not-burn products, that transport liquid or vapor-forming material to a heating element. The classification includes various wick materials, structures and configurations designed to ensure consistent delivery of the substance during use. It falls under the broader categories of human necessities, tobacco and smokers' requisites.
[A24F 40/46] Shape or structure of electric heating means -- A24F 40/46 classifies the geometric design and structural features of electric heating elements used in simulated smoking devices such as e-cigarettes. It covers the shape, dimensions, arrangement and integration of coils, plates or other heating means that vaporise liquid, focusing on how these configurations affect performance and user experience.
[A24F 40/465] specially adapted for induction heating -- A24F 40/465 covers simulated-smoking devices that are specifically designed for induction heating, where the heating element is energized by an electromagnetic field rather than a direct electrical coil or battery. It includes e-cigarettes, heated-tobacco products and related accessories whose operation relies on inductive energy transfer to generate aerosol.
[A24F 40/48] Fluid transfer means, e.g. pumps -- A24F 40/48 covers the fluid-transfer components, such as pumps or similar mechanisms, used in simulated smoking devices. It includes any structure that moves liquid or vapor from a reservoir to the heating element or mouthpiece to generate and deliver the aerosol.
[A24F 40/485] Valves -- The A24F 40/485 subclass covers valves incorporated in simulated smoking devices such as electronic cigarettes or other non-combustion nicotine delivery systems. It includes designs that control airflow, liquid vaporisation, or puff-activation, and may address refillable or disposable valve mechanisms used to regulate inhalation and prevent leakage.
[A24F 40/49] Child proofing -- A24F 40/49 pertains to child-proofing measures for simulated smoking devices, covering designs and mechanisms that prevent children from opening or using such products, including child-resistant packaging, safety locks and tamper-evident features. This subclass falls under Human Necessities → Tobacco → Smokers' Requisites → Simulated smoking devices.
[A24F 40/50] Control or monitoring -- A24F 40/50 covers control or monitoring features for simulated smoking devices, including electronic cigarettes and similar products. It includes mechanisms that regulate, track or adjust parameters such as activation, temperature, puff count, aerosol generation or user-feedback, often using sensors, microprocessors or remote-control interfaces.
[A24F 40/51] Arrangement of sensors -- A24F 40/51 covers the arrangement of sensors in simulated smoking devices, such as electronic cigarettes or heat-not-burn products. It relates to the placement, orientation and integration of components that detect puff count, airflow, temperature, pressure or battery status to control the device's operation. This subclass groups inventions that focus on how these sensing elements are organized within the smoking-aid apparatus.
[A24F 40/53] Monitoring, e.g. fault detection -- A24F 40/53 concerns monitoring functions for simulated smoking devices, such as electronic cigarettes, focusing on fault detection, performance diagnostics, and safety checks. It covers sensors, control circuits, software algorithms, and reporting mechanisms that continuously assess device operation and alert users or manufacturers to malfunctions. This subclass ensures reliable and safe use of these tobacco-related products.
[A24F 40/57] Temperature control -- A24F 40/57 covers technologies that regulate the temperature of simulated smoking devices such as e-cigarettes or heated tobacco products, including heating elements, sensors, control circuits and methods for maintaining a set temperature during operation. This classification falls within Human Necessities → Tobacco → Smokers' Requisites → Simulated Smoking Devices.
[A24F 40/60] Devices with integrated user interfaces -- A24F 40/60 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers simulated smoking devices that feature integrated user interfaces, enabling users to interact directly with the device for control or customization. This classification is specific to devices designed to mimic traditional smoking experiences while incorporating modern technological elements.
[A24F 40/65] Devices with integrated communication means, e.g. wireless communication means -- This subclass covers simulated smoking devices, such as e-cigarettes or vaping units, that incorporate built-in communication functions - for example Bluetooth, Wi-Fi or other wireless links - enabling data exchange, remote control, usage monitoring or interaction with external devices.
[A24F 40/70] Manufacture -- The CPC subclass A24F 40/70 covers methods and apparatus for manufacturing simulated smoking devices such as e-cigarettes and related products. It sits within the hierarchy human necessities - tobacco - smokers' requisites - simulated smoking devices. The entry addresses the production processes, assembly and fabrication steps for these devices.
[A24F 40/80] Testing -- A24F 40/80 covers methods and apparatus for evaluating simulated smoking devices, such as electronic cigarettes or other nicotine-delivery systems. The classification includes procedures for measuring aerosol generation, nicotine yield, mouth-to-lung delivery, battery performance and other functional parameters that determine product behaviour under use conditions. It is used to document testing techniques aimed at assessing safety, efficacy and regulatory compliance.
[A24F 40/85] Maintenance, e.g. cleaning -- The classification covers methods, apparatuses, and procedures for maintaining and cleaning simulated smoking devices such as electronic cigarettes, heat-not-burn units, and other tobacco-free inhalers. It includes routine cleaning, residue removal, component replacement, and other upkeep tasks that ensure device performance and user safety.
[A24F 40/90] Arrangements or methods specially adapted for charging batteries thereof -- The code A24F 40/90 covers specific arrangements or methods for charging the battery of simulated smoking devices such as electronic cigarettes, including connector designs, charging stations, and circuitry adapted to safely recharge the device's power source. It addresses features that facilitate efficient, controlled charging while maintaining device integrity and user safety.
[A24F 40/95] structurally associated with cases -- A24F 40/95 covers structural features of cases used with simulated smoking devices, such as the design, construction and material of housings, protective shells, and accessory enclosures that contain or support the device's functional components. It includes variations in shape, attachment mechanisms, sealing elements and ergonomic fittings that are integral to the case itself.
[A24F 42/00] Simulated smoking devices other than electrically operated -- A24F 42/00 covers non-electrically operated devices that imitate smoking, such as hand-rolled or mechanical cigarettes, pipes, cigars or other apparatuses designed to produce a simulated smoking experience without the use of electricity. These items are classified under the smokers' requisites subclass of tobacco.
[A24F 42/10] Devices with chemical heating means -- A24F 42/10 covers non-electrical simulated smoking devices that use chemical reactions to generate heat for producing smoke-like aerosol. The classification includes products where the heating element is a combustible or exothermic chemical mixture rather than an electric coil or battery. It is applied to items such as herbal or nicotine-containing sticks that are lit by a chemical heat source.
[A24F 42/20] Devices without heating means -- A24F 42/20 covers simulated-smoking devices that provide a smoking-like experience without any heating element, encompassing non-electrically operated products such as purely mechanical or herbal implements meant to imitate smoking. It falls under Human Necessities → Tobacco → Smokers' Requisites → Simulated smoking devices other than electrically operated.
[A24F 42/60] Constructional details -- A24F 42/60 covers the constructional details of simulated smoking devices that are not electrically operated, such as the shape, materials, assembly methods and internal components of pipes, cigars or other non-electrical smoking replicas. It focuses on the physical design features that define how these devices are built and function.

[A24F 42/90] Testing -- A24F 42/90 covers methods and procedures for testing simulated smoking devices that are not electrically powered, such as non-electronic herbal or nicotine-free props. The classification includes performance, safety, and quality-assessment techniques used to evaluate how these devices replicate the act of smoking. It is a sub-category of the CPC hierarchy under Human Necessities → Tobacco → Smokers' Requisites.

[A24F 5/00] Component parts or accessories for pipes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bowls for pipes per se, features thereof.
[A24F 5/02] with arrangements for keeping upright, when put aside -- A24F 5/02 covers pipe accessories that incorporate features to keep the pipe standing upright when it is set aside, such as built-in stands, holders or stabilising mechanisms that prevent the pipe from falling over during storage.
[A24F 5/04] with holes for admitting air -- A24F 5/04 covers pipe components or accessories that incorporate holes or vents to admit air, such as perforated stems, bowls or other parts designed to provide controlled airflow for the smoker. This feature improves draw and may influence combustion, and the subclass includes any pipe element where air-admitting openings are a defining element.
[A24F 5/06] with insets of clay or the like -- A24F 5/06 covers pipe components or accessories that incorporate inserts made of clay or similar ceramic materials, such as insets designed to cool smoke, filter particles, or add flavor. The subclass is part of the smokers' requisites section dealing with pipe parts.
[A24F 5/08] with grates, sieves, or the like -- A24F 5/08 covers pipe components and accessories that incorporate grates, sieves, screens or similar filtering elements, designed to retain tobacco while allowing airflow. This sub-class relates to parts that improve smoking performance by separating solid material from the combustion air stream.
[A24F 5/10] Bowl-covers, attached and removable -- A24F 5/10 covers bowl-covers used on smoking pipes, including both permanently attached and removable types. These items are classified as component parts or accessories for pipes within the broader category of smokers' requisites under human necessities.
[A24F 5/12] with attached stoppers -- The subclass A24F 5/12 covers pipe components or accessories that incorporate a permanently attached stopper, such as stems, mouthpieces or other pipe parts in which the stoppering element is fixed rather than removable. This pertains to tobacco-related smokers' requisites where the stopper is integrated into the part.
[A24F 5/14] with wind screens -- The A24F 5/14 entry designates pipe accessories that incorporate wind screens to shield the tobacco stream from drafts. It is a subcategory of component parts or accessories for pipes within the smokers' requisites section of the human necessities field.
[A24F 7/00] Mouthpieces for pipes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mouthpieces both for pipes and cigarette holders.
[A24F 7/02] with detachable connecting members -- This subclass addresses pipe mouthpieces that include detachable connecting members, enabling the mouthpiece to be separated from the pipe for cleaning, replacement or reconfiguration. It falls under the tobacco accessories section of the Human Necessities group.
[A24F 7/04] with smoke filters -- This subclass includes mouthpieces for smoking pipes that incorporate smoke-filter elements designed to trap particulate matter and reduce harmful constituents in the inhaled smoke. It covers designs where the filter is integrated into the pipe's tip or attached as a replaceable component.

[A24F 9/06] for stems, e.g. brushes, needles, strings -- A24F 9/06 designates pipe-stem accessories such as brushes, needles or strings used for cleaning, repairing or otherwise maintaining a smoker's pipe. [A24F 9/08] Cleaning-sets -- A24F 9/08 covers cleaning sets intended for smokers' pipes, encompassing kits that combine tools such as brushes, rods, swabs and cleaning solutions designed to remove residue and maintain pipe hygiene. These sets are provided as complete accessories for routine pipe maintenance.
[A24F 9/10] for bowls, e.g. scrapers, bowl-cleaners with cutting-teeth -- The A24F 9/10 subgroup covers accessories designed to clean or maintain the bowl of a smoker's pipe, specifically scrapers and bowl-cleaners equipped with cutting teeth that remove residue and buildup.
[A24F 9/12] Devices for cleaning tobacco pipes by steam, air, or water -- Devices that employ steam, air or water to remove residue from tobacco pipes, serving as cleaning accessories for pipe smokers. This class falls under the smokers' requisites segment of the CPC hierarchy, specifically within the "Accessories for smokers' pipes" group.
[A24F 9/14] Stands or supports for tobacco pipes -- A24F 9/14 pertains to accessories that hold or support tobacco pipes, including pipe stands, racks, holders and similar devices designed to keep a pipe stable, protect surfaces and facilitate storage or use. The classification covers both fixed and portable supports made from materials such as wood, metal or plastic.
[A24F 9/16] Protective coverings or cases for pipes or parts thereof -- A24F 9/16 pertains to protective coverings, cases, or similar protective devices intended to enclose whole smoking pipes or individual pipe components such as stems, bowls or mouthpieces, shielding them from damage, dust and wear.
